ballot_results.name: Proposition A
ballot_results.title: Affordable Housing Bonds
ballot_results.description: A $300 million bond measure to fund affordable housing construction and preservation, including acquisition of at-risk buildings and new housing for low-income residents.

ballot_results.name: Proposition B
ballot_results.title: Community Health & Safety Bonds
ballot_results.description: A $390 million bond for community health and safety infrastructure, including hospital upgrades, fire stations, emergency shelters, and family housing facilities.

ballot_results.name: Proposition C
ballot_results.title: Inspector General
ballot_results.description: Creates an independent Inspector General position to investigate fraud, waste, and abuse in city government, with subpoena power and reporting requirements.

ballot_results.name: Proposition D
ballot_results.title: Reduce Commissions, Expand Mayoral Power
ballot_results.description: Would have reduced the number of city commissions by roughly half and given the Mayor greater power to appoint and remove commissioners.

ballot_results.name: Proposition E
ballot_results.title: Commission Reform Task Force
ballot_results.description: Establishes a task force to evaluate the city's commission structure and recommend reforms, as an alternative to the more aggressive Prop D.

ballot_results.name: Proposition F
ballot_results.title: Police Deferred Retirement
ballot_results.description: Allows police officers eligible for retirement to defer it and continue working for up to 5 years while drawing partial pension benefits, aimed at retaining experienced officers.

ballot_results.name: Proposition G
ballot_results.title: Affordable Housing Rental Subsidies
ballot_results.description: Creates the Affordable Housing Opportunity Fund to provide rental subsidies for extremely low-income households, funded at $8.25 million annually.

ballot_results.name: Proposition H
ballot_results.title: Firefighter Retirement Age
ballot_results.description: Lowers the maximum retirement eligibility age for firefighters from 58 to 55, aligning with neighboring fire departments to improve recruitment.

ballot_results.name: Proposition I
ballot_results.title: Nurses & 911 Dispatcher Pensions
ballot_results.description: Improves pension benefits for 911 dispatchers and nurses employed by the city to improve recruitment and retention for these critical roles.

ballot_results.name: Proposition J
ballot_results.title: Children & Youth Fund Oversight
ballot_results.description: Strengthens oversight of the Children and Youth Fund by creating accountability mechanisms for how the city spends money on youth programs.

ballot_results.name: Proposition K
ballot_results.title: Close Upper Great Highway
ballot_results.description: Permanently closes the Upper Great Highway to private vehicles, converting it into a public oceanfront recreation space and park.

ballot_results.name: Proposition L
ballot_results.title: Rideshare & AV Tax for Muni
ballot_results.description: Imposes a 1% to 4.5% gross receipts tax on rideshare and autonomous vehicle companies operating in SF, generating ~$25M annually for Muni.

ballot_results.name: Proposition M
ballot_results.title: Business Tax Reform
ballot_results.description: Major business tax reform restructuring 14 tax categories into 7, shifting from payroll-based to revenue-based taxation and raising the small business exemption to $5M.

ballot_results.name: Proposition N
ballot_results.title: First Responder Student Loan Aid
ballot_results.description: Provides student loan repayment assistance for first responders (police, firefighters, paramedics) to attract new recruits amid staffing shortages.

ballot_results.name: Proposition O
ballot_results.title: Reproductive Healthcare Access
ballot_results.description: Strengthens access to reproductive healthcare including abortion, protects patient and provider privacy, and funds reproductive health services.

ballot_results.name: Proposition A
ballot_results.title: Infrastructure Bonds
ballot_results.description: Infrastructure bonds for city facilities including roads, public transit, and emergency response systems.

ballot_results.name: Proposition B
ballot_results.title: Police Minimum Staffing
ballot_results.description: Would have increased the mandatory minimum police staffing level from 1,700 to 2,074 officers. Defeated with 67% opposition.

ballot_results.name: Proposition C
ballot_results.title: Real Estate Transfer Tax Exemption
ballot_results.description: Exempts commercial-to-residential building conversions from the real estate transfer tax to incentivize creating housing from empty office buildings.

ballot_results.name: Proposition D
ballot_results.title: Ethics Law Changes
ballot_results.description: Expanded ethics law restrictions on gifts to city officials and strengthened conflict-of-interest rules.

ballot_results.name: Proposition E
ballot_results.title: Expanded Police Powers
ballot_results.description: Expanded police powers including use of drones, surveillance cameras, and reduced administrative task time for officers.

ballot_results.name: Proposition F
ballot_results.title: Drug Screening for Welfare
ballot_results.description: Requires drug screening as a condition of receiving county cash welfare assistance, with mandatory treatment referrals for those who test positive.

ballot_results.name: Proposition G
ballot_results.title: Algebra by 8th Grade
ballot_results.description: Non-binding policy statement urging SFUSD to offer algebra instruction to all students by eighth grade.

ballot_results.name: Proposition A
ballot_results.title: Retiree Benefits Funding
ballot_results.description: Adjusts the funding formula for city retiree health and pension benefits to reflect updated actuarial projections.

ballot_results.name: Proposition D
ballot_results.title: Affordable Housing Streamlining
ballot_results.description: Would have removed Board of Supervisors approval requirements for certain affordable housing projects to speed up construction.

ballot_results.name: Proposition E
ballot_results.title: Housing Project Approval
ballot_results.description: Required Board of Supervisors approval for housing projects in certain areas, as a counter-proposal to Prop D's streamlining approach.

ballot_results.name: Proposition F
ballot_results.title: Library Preservation Fund
ballot_results.description: Renews the Library Preservation Fund for 25 years, providing approximately 97% of the public library system's annual operating budget.

ballot_results.name: Proposition I
ballot_results.title: Cars on JFK Drive
ballot_results.description: Would have restored private vehicle access to JFK Drive in Golden Gate Park on weekdays, reversing the pandemic-era car-free policy.

ballot_results.name: Proposition J
ballot_results.title: JFK Drive Car-Free
ballot_results.description: Maintains JFK Drive in Golden Gate Park as permanently car-free for pedestrians, cyclists, and recreational use.

ballot_results.name: Proposition L
ballot_results.title: Sales Tax for Transportation
ballot_results.description: Extends a 0.5% city sales tax for 30 years with authorization for up to $1.91 billion in bonds dedicated to transportation improvements.

ballot_results.name: Proposition M
ballot_results.title: Vacancy Tax
ballot_results.description: Imposes a tax on owners of residential properties that have been vacant for more than 182 days per year, to discourage housing speculation.

ballot_results.name: Proposition N
ballot_results.title: Golden Gate Park Parking
ballot_results.description: Authorizes construction of an underground parking garage in Golden Gate Park to replace surface parking displaced by the JFK Drive closure.
